message:
fix(combinator): implement actual cancel masking in commit_section

commit_section and try_commit_section previously called cx.masked()
with an empty closure and then awaited the future unmasked — the cancel
mask had no effect on the actual computation. Now both functions box-pin
the inner future and poll it inside cx.masked() via poll_fn, ensuring
every poll of the inner future runs under the cancel mask.

This means cx.checkpoint() calls within a commit section will not
observe cancellation, which is the intended semantic: commit sections
represent atomic work that must complete once started.

New tests: PendingThenCheckpoint futures verify that checkpoint()
succeeds (returns Ok) inside a commit section even when cancellation
is requested on the Cx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
